technology: I/O-bound data processing on constrained resources
abstract: A practitioner reference for processing datasets larger than RAM on a single low-compute box. 41 rules across 9 categories — memory discipline, I/O access patterns, data format & encoding, chunking & batching, spill-to-disk, pipelining & backpressure, compression & serialization, concurrency for I/O-bound workloads, and observability — organized by execution-lifecycle impact and the cascade effect, with incorrect/correct Python examples and pointers to canonical sources (Apache Arrow/Parquet, Polars, DuckDB, pandas, Linux man pages, Brendan Gregg's Systems Performance, Designing Data-Intensive Applications, zstd/lz4 benchmarks). Designed to complement the algorithmic primitives in computer-science-algorithms with the I/O-pragmatic patterns needed to actually move bytes through a constrained box.
